title: Website hub foundation launched
summary: temitayocharles.online now has routed website sections for projects, news, writing, studies, lab, and GitHub activity.
detailSummary: This release moves the site from a single-scroll portfolio toward an editorial and product narrative hub with durable route structure.

bodySections.title: What shipped
bodySections.body: Dedicated hub routes are now available for projects, updates, writing, studies, lab, and curated engineering digest surfaces.

bodySections.title: Why it matters
bodySections.body: The route model improves discoverability and supports focused public communication without requiring backend data for first render.

takeaways: Route-level organization is now a first-class publishing surface.
Public content remains static-first and deployment-safe.
The hub can expand editorially without changing homepage anchors.
safetyNote: Public-safe update: no private repository names or sensitive implementation details are included.
impact: Moves the site from a one-page portfolio toward a public operating hub for InfraForge, AI infrastructure, platform engineering, and product work.

title: AI Inference Lab: model operations laboratory published
summary: The InfraForge AI Inference Lab case study is live. Covers LLM serving, model operations, GPU capacity planning, benchmarking, Prometheus metrics, and Kubernetes-native AI workloads.
detailSummary: The public case-study surface now explains model-serving operations as an engineering discipline rather than a demo-only workflow.

bodySections.title: Operational scope
bodySections.body: Coverage includes serving boundaries, metrics posture, benchmark reporting, and workload progression toward Kubernetes-native operation.

bodySections.title: Publication posture
bodySections.body: The published material is intentionally high-signal and public-safe, avoiding sensitive runtime topology disclosures.

takeaways: AI infrastructure is treated as platform operations, not prompt demos.
Measurement and observability are central to credibility.
Public documentation remains curated and security-aware.
impact: Establishes the AI infrastructure proof surface for InfraForge as a serious production-capacity system.

title: ForgeWatch AIOps copilot case study published
summary: ForgeWatch evidence-first operations copilot case study is live. Covers read-only Kubernetes/Prometheus/Loki evidence gathering, approval-gated write proposals, and outcome-backed RAG memory.
detailSummary: This publication frames ForgeWatch as an evidence-first guardian layer with human approval boundaries.

bodySections.title: Operating principles
bodySections.body: Read-first evidence gathering and approval-gated write recommendations remain the core governance pattern.

bodySections.title: Public communication
bodySections.body: The page explains decision quality and operator safety without exposing internal incident payloads or private repository context.

takeaways: Operational intelligence is useful only when evidence quality is high.
Public messaging should emphasize governance, not hidden internals.
impact: Positions ForgeWatch as the operational intelligence and cluster guardian layer for InfraForge.

title: Legal pages live: Trust & Safety, Privacy Policy, Terms
summary: Trust & Safety, Privacy Policy, and Terms pages are now live at /trust-safety, /privacy-policy, and /terms.
detailSummary: The public hub now includes baseline trust and policy surfaces required for credible external product communication.

bodySections.title: What changed
bodySections.body: Dedicated legal and policy routes are now part of the navigation and route-verification baseline.

bodySections.title: Why this matters
bodySections.body: Trust surfaces clarify expectations for privacy, safety, and acceptable use while keeping policy language aligned with public operation.

takeaways: Compliance posture is now visible on public routes.
Legal pages are included in route monitoring.
impact: Completes the compliance and trust surface for the public brand hub.

title: Project Iris governed personal AI assistant case study published
summary: Project Iris case study is live. Covers approval queues, governed memory, MCP gateway, email/calendar/browser workflows, audit service, and execution worker.
detailSummary: The public case study positions Iris as a governed personal assistant with explicit control boundaries and audit-conscious workflows.

bodySections.title: Capability framing
bodySections.body: Iris is framed as an operator-supporting personal assistant, not an unrestricted autonomous engineering system.

bodySections.title: Safety framing
bodySections.body: Approval queues and controlled actions are highlighted as first-class product requirements.

takeaways: Governance and usability can coexist in assistant workflows.
Control boundaries are part of product quality.
Public narratives should prioritize trust over hype.
impact: Establishes Iris as a premium personal AI pattern: capable, governed, and trust-preserving.

title: InfraForge platform operating model case study published
summary: The InfraForge company platform case study is live. Covers GitOps, Vault-backed secrets, Envoy Gateway, Longhorn, Harbor, Prometheus/Grafana/Loki, and disaster recovery.
detailSummary: The case study clarifies InfraForge as an operating model with reliability, governance, and delivery controls.

bodySections.title: System posture
bodySections.body: The public narrative centers on delivery consistency, observability, and resilient operations instead of tool listing.

bodySections.title: Evidence posture
bodySections.body: Published details remain high-level and product-safe while still showing practical engineering depth.

takeaways: Platform engineering quality is communicated through operating model clarity.
Reliability and recovery are core brand proof points.
Public-safe summaries can still be technically credible.
impact: Demonstrates InfraForge as a real platform company with an owned operating model.
